reason: Adding the noexec mount option modifies the filesystem mount configuration for /home, which can prevent execution of binaries stored there. This change may break legitimate user applications, scripts, or services that rely on executable files in /home and typically requires a remount or reboot, introducing risk of service disruption. According to policy, filesystem/partitioning changes are classified as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; Potential breakage of user applications that rely on executable files in /home; Possible denial of service for users if required binaries cannot execute; Requires remount or reboot, which may interrupt services
